overall.description: A ship simulation game showing a first-person view from a vessel's bridge at night. The camera looks forward over the ship's bow through rain-streaked windows, with three navigation monitors displaying radar, charts, and sonar data as the vessel moves through dark waters.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge with navigation consoles overlooking bow

events.description: rain begins to streak across the bridge windows, reducing visibility slightly
ship begins a slight turn to port, visible on the radar display

agents.ego_description: operating the ship's controls from the bridge, monitoring navigation displays while moving through coastal waters at night

entered.detail: digital chart display showing coastal map with ship position marker
circular radar display with rotating yellow sweep showing surrounding terrain
blue sonar or depth sounder display showing underwater topography
white painted bow of the vessel with red navigation lights, visible through the bridge windows
metal safety railing along the port side of the foredeck
metal safety railing along the starboard side of the foredeck
dark choppy water illuminated by the ship's forward lighting

segments.scene_description: The view from a ship's bridge at night, looking forward over the vessel's bow as it moves through dark waters. Three navigation monitors display radar, charts, and sonar data overhead. Rain intermittently streaks across the windows, and the ship executes a slight turn visible on the radar display while maintaining steady forward motion.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot captures a steady first-person perspective from a ship's bridge as the vessel navigates through a dark, rainy night. It begins with a forward-facing view over the bow, where the dark waters are intermittently obscured by rain streaking across the windows. As the ship maintains its forward momentum, the focus shifts slightly to the overhead navigation monitors displaying active radar, charts, and sonar data. The scene then evolves as the vessel executes a gradual turn, a movement that is mirrored in real-time on the radar display before the camera settles back into its steady course through the gloom.
